content.claim: Poland's 1999 educational reform, which delayed tracking by extending primary education from 8 to 6 years and introducing a comprehensive 3-year middle tier (gimnazjum), was followed by PISA gains that placed Poland in the top 10 in reading and science, with the largest improvements concentrated among students who would previously have entered vocational tracks.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Polish system reform lens
limitations: PISA reflects only academic achievement; no data on vocational outcomes or broader labor market effects are provided
Attribution of gains solely to the delay in selection is contested; multiple confounding factors (curriculum changes, teaching quality improvements, economic growth) may have contributed
Secondary source synthesis (Grokipedia) cited for labor market claims without primary source specification
2015+ cohort data does not permit assessment of full implementation effects
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
